If it’s an instance of `datetime.datetime`, asset\_type (str")) – The asset type of requested data. It must be one of `'spot'`, `'futures/um'`, `'futures/cm'`. data\_type (str")) – The type of requested data. It must be one of `'klines'`, `'agg_trades'`. it’s timezone is ignored. If it’s a `str`, it should be parsed by dateutil, e.g. `"2022-1-18:10"`. end (datetime")) – [...] Parameters: : symbol (str")) – start (str") | datetime")) – end (str") | datetime")) – asset\_type (str")) – tz (str") | None) – Return type: : DataFrame") [...] The end datetime of requested data. If it’s an instance of `datetime.datetime`, it’s timezone is ignored. If it’s a `str`, it should be parsed by dateutil, e.g. `"2022-1-28:10"`. tz (str") | None) – Timezone of `start`, `end`, and the open/close datetime of the returned dataframe. evidences.title: Binance.US API Documentation
evidences.content: + Previously some requests failed if the `startTime` == `endTime`. + Now, all API requests that accept `startTime` and `endTime` allow the parameters to be equal. This applies to the following requests: - `GET /api/v3/aggTrades` - `GET /api/v3/klines` - `GET /api/v3/allOrderList` - `GET /api/v3/allOrders` - `GET /api/v3/myTrades` Changes to Filter Evaluation: + Previous behavior: `LOT_SIZE` and `MARKET_LOT_SIZE` required that (`quantity` - `minQty`) % `stepSize` == 0. + New behavior changed to: (`quantity` % `stepSize`) == 0. Bug fix with reverse `MARKET` orders (i.e. `MARKET` using `quoteOrderQty`): [...] Fixed error message for querying archived orders(status `CANCELED` or `EXPIRED` where `executedQty` == 0 in the last 90 days): + Previous error message: `{` `"code": -2013,` `"msg": "Order does not exist."` `}` + Now error message: `{` `"code": -2026,` `"msg": "Order was canceled or expired with no executed qty over 90 days ago and has been archived."` `}` Behavior for API requests with `startTime` and `endTime`: [...] > Response ``` [{" a ": 874844, " p ": "0.00379700 ", " q ": "0.05000000 ", " f ": 981493, " l ": 981493, " T ": 1637128220041, " m ": true, " M ": true}] ``` `...

evidences.title: Save Historical data from Binance
evidences.content: `symbol` and `limit` are straightforward, `startTime` and `endTime` we now have covered by the above `date_to_milliseconds` function. We note from this that we can get a maximum of 500 results each request, so we will need to build a loop if we are fetching over a long time period. `interval` we have a list of so these are passed easily. The get\_klines endpoint returns an array of klines in this order ``` [1499040000000, # Open time "0.01634790", # Open "0.80000000", # High "0.01575800", # Low "0.01577100", # Close "148976.11427815", # Volume 1499644799999, # Close time "2434.19055334", # Quote asset volume 308, # Number of trades "1756.87402397", # Taker buy base asset volume "28.46694368", # Taker buy quote asset volume "17928899.62484339" # Ignore ] ``` [...] `get_historical_klines` has been added to the binance.client, so now all you need to do is run ``` import json frombinance.client import Client client = Client("", "") klines = client. get_historical_klines("ETHBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_30MINUTE,"1 Dec, 2017","1 Jan, 2018") ``` ### Next Steps With these cached klines we can open them at a later date and run backtesting on them. See the related [Kucoin post][kucoin-po...

evidences.title: Binance.US API Documentation: Introduction
evidences.content: + Previously some requests failed if the `startTime` == `endTime`. + Now, all API requests that accept `startTime` and `endTime` allow the parameters to be equal. This applies to the following requests: - `GET /api/v3/aggTrades` - `GET /api/v3/klines` - `GET /api/v3/allOrderList` - `GET /api/v3/allOrders` - `GET /api/v3/myTrades` Changes to Filter Evaluation: + Previous behavior: `LOT_SIZE` and `MARKET_LOT_SIZE` required that (`quantity` - `minQty`) % `stepSize` == 0. + New behavior changed to: (`quantity` % `stepSize`) == 0. Bug fix with reverse `MARKET` orders (i.e. `MARKET` using `quoteOrderQty`): [...] New error code -1135 occurs if a parameter requiring a JSON object is invalid. New error code -1108 occurs if a value sent to a parameter is too large. Changes to GET /api/v3/aggTrades + startTime and endTime can now be used individually and the 1-hour limit has been removed. Changes to GET /api/v3/myTrades + Bug fixed: The combination of symbol + orderId no longer returns all trades beyond the 500 default limit. + Sending an unsupported combination of optional parameters now responds with generic error: { “code”: -1128, “msg”: “Combination of optional parameters invalid.” }....

evidences.title: ET to UTC Converter
evidences.content: Time Difference: UTC is 5 hours ahead of EST (winter) and 4 hours ahead of EDT (summer). The difference changes twice yearly with daylight saving transitions. #### Daylight Saving Time Impact Winter Period (EST): From first Sunday in November to second Sunday in March, ET is UTC-5, making UTC 5 hours ahead Summer Period (EDT): From second Sunday in March to first Sunday in November, ET is UTC-4, making UTC 4 hours ahead UTC Stability: UTC never changes and remains constant year-round, making it ideal for international coordination and technical applications #### Common Conversion Examples Business Hours (Winter) EST 9:00 AM → UTC 2:00 PM EST 5:00 PM → UTC 10:00 PM 5-hour difference during winter Business Hours (Summer) EDT 9:00 AM → UTC 1:00 PM EDT 5:00 PM → UTC 9:00 PM [...] ### Example: When you’re working across borders Say you're a project manager in Toronto (ET), coordinating with a dev team in Ghana (UTC). You want to schedule a meeting at 10:00 AM your time. With this tool, you instantly see that it will be 3:00 PM in Ghana during winter, or 2:00 PM in summer when daylight saving is in effect. That kind of clarity can make or break a successful sprint review. ## Handle yo...

evidences.title: EDT to UTC Converter - World Time Buddy
evidences.content: | Converting EDT to UTC This time zone converter lets you visually and very quickly convert EDT to UTC and vice-versa. Simply mouse over the colored hour-tiles and glance at the hours selected by the column... and done! EDT stands for Eastern Daylight Time. UTC is known as Universal Time. UTC is 4 hours ahead of EDT. So, when it is 12:00 am EDT1:00 am EDT2:00 am EDT3:00 am EDT4:00 am EDT5:00 am EDT6:00 am EDT7:00 am EDT8:00 am EDT9:00 am EDT10:00 am EDT11:00 am EDT12:00 pm EDT1:00 pm EDT2:00 pm EDT3:00 pm EDT4:00 pm EDT5:00 pm EDT6:00 pm EDT7:00 pm EDT8:00 pm EDT9:00 pm EDT10:00 pm EDT11:00 pm EDT it will be 4:00 am UTC5:00 am UTC6:00 am UTC7:00 am UTC8:00 am UTC9:00 am UTC10:00 am UTC11:00 am UTC12:00 pm UTC1:00 pm UTC2:00 pm UTC3:00 pm UTC4:00 pm UTC5:00 pm UTC6:00 pm UTC7:00 pm [...] | Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) to Universal Time (UTC) | | | --- | 12 pm EDT | is | 4 pm UTC | | 1 pm EDT | is | 5 pm UTC | | 2 pm EDT | is | 6 pm UTC | | 3 pm EDT | is | 7 pm UTC | | 4 pm EDT | is | 8 pm UTC | | 5 pm EDT | is | 9 pm UTC | | 6 pm EDT | is | 10 pm UTC | | 7 pm EDT | is | 11 pm UTC | | 8 pm EDT | is | 12 am UTC | | 9 pm EDT | is | 1 am UTC | | 10 pm EDT | is | 2 am UTC | | 11 pm EDT...

Copied embed code to clipboard! ## Frequently Asked Questions ### How do you convert Eastern Time to UTC? UTC is 5 hours ahead of EST or 4 hours ahead of EDT ### What is the time difference between Eastern Time and UTC? The time difference between Eastern Time and Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) depends on whether Daylight Saving Time is in effect. During standard time (EST), UTC is 5 hours ahead. During daylight time (EDT), UTC is 4 hours ahead.
